# Insurance Agency Acquisition Guide 2026 — TheDealSheet

**Author:** TheDealSheet Editorial
**Source:** [thedealsheet.co](https://thedealsheet.co/industries/insurance)
**Saved:** 2026-04-20

## Why I Saved This

Specialty Insurance Brokerage is an active G&B niche (broadened 4/19 to Fine Art + Collectibles + Jewelry + Wine + Classic Auto + Yacht + Aviation + Equine + Cyber UHNW + D&O family office + Museum + Auction — target pool ~500-1,500). TDS industry page is generic P&C and Employee Benefits focused, NOT specialty-line segmented, but contains transferable benchmarks (valuation bands, M&A velocity, named PE platforms, cyber CAGR) relevant to G&B's variation. This snapshot preserves TDS's generic-P&C POV alongside explicit contrast against G&B's specialty thesis so future agents can see where the variation holds and where it needs sharpening.

## Content

### Market Size & Growth
- U.S. market: $261.7B (2025, IBISWorld)
- CAGR: 4.14% through 2030 (Mordor Intelligence)
- M&A volume: 695 deals YTD 2025 (down 12% YoY per OPTIS Partners)
- PE-backed buyers = 72% of all acquisitions

### Revenue Segmentation
- P&C Brokerage: 66%
- Employee Benefits & Group: 13%
- Life & Annuity: 12%
- **Specialty Lines & MGA: 9%** (5.92% CAGR, outpacing base P&C)

### Valuation Multiples

| Revenue Band | Multiple | Metric |
|---|---|---|
| $500K-$1.25M | 2.8x-3.2x | SDE |
| $1.25M-$5M | 3.2x-3.8x | SDE |
| $5M-$10M | 3.5x-4.1x | SDE |
| $10M-$25M | 4.0x-4.5x | SDE |
| $25M+ | 7.0x-9.0x | EBITDA |

Median owner's discretionary earnings: $195K. Median sale price: $650K.

**Premium multiple drivers (4.0x-6.0x):** cyber/specialty lines ≥25% of revenue, fee-based advisory ≥20%, 90%+ retention, modern tech stack (AMS360, Applied Epic, Salesforce), producer depth, benefits book with F500 anchors.

**Discount triggers (2.0x-2.5x):** owner-dependency, top-5 concentration >40%, commission-only, legacy tech, <3% organic growth, single-line P&C.

### Named PE Platforms

| Platform | Sponsor | 2025 Deals | Focus |
|---|---|---|---|
| BroadStreet Partners | PE-backed | 57 YTD (leader) | Regional P&C |
| Hub International | PE-backed | 38-49 YTD | **Multi-line + HNW private client (750 advisors, 70K HNW clients)** |
| Hilb Group | Carlyle | 200+ cumulative | P&C + benefits, multi-state |
| King Risk Partners | PE-backed | Multiple tuck-ins | P&C geographic |
| Arthur J. Gallagher | Public/Strategic | Large deals | Wholesale/specialty |

M&A trajectory: 2022 ~100 → 2023 ~100 → 2024 138 → 2025 695 on pace.

### Unit Economics by Line

| Service | Avg Ticket | Commission/Margin |
|---|---|---|
| Personal P&C | $1.2K/yr | 15-20% |
| Commercial P&C SMB | $8K-$25K/yr | 15-25% |
| Employee Benefits | $50K-$500K/yr | 3-6% + fees |
| **Cyber** | $3K-$15K/yr | **20-30%** |
| Fee-Based Advisory | $500-$1K/mo | 40-50% net |

### Industry KPIs

| Metric | Benchmark | Top Quartile |
|---|---|---|
| Client retention | 85-90% | 95%+ |
| Organic growth | 5-7% | 10.7% |
| EBITDA margin | 18-22% | 26.1% |
| Revenue per producer | $350K-$500K | $750K+ |
| Top-5 concentration | 25-35% | <20% |

### Labor
47K open positions, 14% turnover, avg wage $60K, 4% YoY wage growth. 89% of new agents quit within 3 years. ~50% workforce retirement projected by 2028. 400K retirements by 2026.

### Specialty Line Callouts (TDS)
- **Cyber:** 27% CAGR through 2026, 58% of insurers using AI in underwriting, ransomware up 41% YoY (FBI)
- **Employee benefits:** DOL fiduciary rule driving 20-25% of income to fee-based consulting
- **Specialty & MGA (9% of market):** 5.92% CAGR, "personal risk management, specialty commercial" named as drivers — **but TDS does NOT break out fine art, collectibles, jewelry, wine, classic auto, aviation, equine, or HNW private client as discrete sub-lines**

### Risks Flagged
Rate softening (P&C down 8-10% preferred risks), combined-ratio worsening (97.2% → 99% 2024-2026), talent crisis, AI governance complexity, legacy tech debt, consolidation pressure on sub-$1.25M independents (30K+ aging), cyber & data security (median ransomware demand $2.73M).

### Tailwinds
Cyber acceleration, fee-based shift, AI/automation (30% admin reduction, 90% faster underwriting), specialty lines 5.92% CAGR, mid-market 11.3% organic growth, PE capital appetite, embedded insurance ($116.5B), 90% of insurers expect more M&A in 2026 (Deloitte).

## Key Patterns

### GENERIC P&C patterns (TDS's frame — applies to broad market, not G&B-specific)
- Pattern G1: **$5-10M revenue is the PE platform sweet spot** (3.5x-4.1x SDE). G&B's sub-$3M focus sits below PE platform interest — this is the G&B arbitrage window, not a validation of the thesis.
- Pattern G2: **BroadStreet/Hilb/King Risk are regional P&C consolidators** — they absorb generalist agencies, not specialty/HNW private-client shops. Competitive overlap with G&B's variation is LOW.
- Pattern G3: **30,000+ sub-$1.25M aging independents** creating "decade-long acquisition pipeline" — the succession wave is real but the TDS framing assumes generic P&C. G&B's specialty slice of that pipeline is smaller but less contested.
- Pattern G4: **Fee-based advisory conversion is the TDS-named value-creation playbook** ("acquire commission-heavy shops at 3.0x-3.5x SDE, layer in fees, expand multiple 0.5x-1.0x in 18-24 months"). This is a PE add-on thesis, not a succession-acquisition thesis — different operator DNA than G&B's.

### SPECIALTY angles (where G&B's variation sharpens TDS)
- Pattern S1: **Hub International is the one named PE platform that actively hunts HNW private client** (750 advisors, 70K HNW clients, 500K mass affluent). Hub is G&B's primary platform competitor in the variation — not BroadStreet/Hilb/King Risk. This is a critical distinction TDS's generic page does NOT surface.
- Pattern S2: **TDS's "specialty & MGA 9% / 5.92% CAGR" slice is the home address of G&B's thesis.** TDS doesn't segment within that 9% — fine art, jewelry, wine, classic auto, aviation, equine, museum, auction all live inside that bucket. The "25%+ specialty-revenue premium → 4.0x-6.0x multiple" rule from TDS applies to G&B's targets with force.
- Pattern S3: **Compliance burden as moat is UNDER-SURFACED by TDS.** TDS treats compliance as a cost line ($2K-$8K/yr). In specialty lines (fine art appraisal standards, Customs for aviation, FINRA/SEC adjacency for family office D&O, auction-house E&O, wine cellar temperature-deviation claims) compliance is a *relationship* moat that generic P&C roll-ups can't absorb without in-house specialty underwriters. G&B's thesis treats this as durable; TDS treats it as operating drag.
- Pattern S4: **Relationship-driven vs price-driven.** TDS treats P&C retention as a function of renewal mechanics and tech stack (95%+ = top quartile). Specialty/HNW retention is driven by trust, discretion, and claims-handling reputation — structurally higher than 95% in intact books but invisible to TDS's generic framing.
- Pattern S5: **Single-line concentration is scored as a discount in TDS (2.0x-2.5x).** In G&B's variation, single-line specialty focus (e.g., a pure fine-art E&O shop) can be the *premium* because it signals deep underwriting expertise, carrier relationships, and non-substitutability. TDS's rule reverses for specialty.

### BLIND SPOTS — TDS data points G&B thesis may not yet incorporate

- Blind spot B1: **Cyber UHNW growing at 27% CAGR** is listed in G&B's broadened sub-industry set but G&B's one-pager likely treats it as a co-equal sub-line. It deserves its own narrative — it's the single fastest-growing sub-line in the TDS universe and the one non-P&C flavor most owners of G&B targets will already be upselling.
- Blind spot B2: **$195K median owner's discretionary earnings / $650K median sale price** anchors G&B's sub-$3M revenue buy box at roughly $500K-$1M purchase range. G&B scorecard should ensure sub-$3M targets clear the $195K SDE floor — below that the deal is too small to absorb integration cost.
- Blind spot B3: **90% of insurers expect more M&A in 2026 (Deloitte).** Specifically applied to G&B's variation: this likely means Hub + specialty-MGA consolidators (AmWINS, Risk Strategies, Alliant) will continue pricing up specialty books during G&B's search window. Valuation ceiling in G&B's buy box may drift from 3.2x-3.8x SDE toward 4.0x-4.5x SDE in 12-18 months. G&B's fiscal year timing matters.
- Blind spot B4: **Embedded insurance = $116.5B and growing.** Not on G&B's radar as a sub-line — but specialty embedded (e.g., jewelry retailer embedded coverage, auction-house embedded transit) could be a bolt-on category. Worth flagging for future niche-intelligence pass.
- Blind spot B5: **89% of new agents quit within 3 years, 47K open positions.** G&B's variation is especially vulnerable because specialty underwriters are rarer than generalist producers. Operator succession in a specialty book is HARDER than generic P&C — if the owner IS the book, there's no one to sell to. This sharpens G&B's "institutional client relationships, not owner-is-the-book" filter from Sam's briefing.
- Blind spot B6: **NY has no license reciprocity + compliance costs 40% higher + PE saturation = "markets to avoid"** per TDS. G&B's Sam brief explicitly plans a NY office post-acquisition. This doesn't invalidate the Midwest-first, NY-expansion thesis (UHNW client concentration in NY metro is real), but TDS flags that NY-domiciled acquisitions carry a structural discount — G&B should acquire Midwest cheap and *open* NY presence, not acquire a NY agency.

## How I Might Use This

- **Verdict: variation still holds.** TDS's generic page does not contain data that kills G&B's specialty thesis; on every specialty-differentiated dimension (compliance moat, relationship stickiness, concentration reframed as premium, Hub as the single meaningful competitor), G&B's lens is sharper than TDS's. The 5.92% CAGR specialty slice + 4.0x-6.0x premium multiple for 25%+ specialty-revenue mix are the most useful transferable numbers.
- **Sharpen G&B's one-pager with:** Hub International call-out as primary platform competitor (not BroadStreet/Hilb); cyber UHNW as the 27%-CAGR star sub-line; $195K SDE floor for sub-$3M buy box; 12-18 month valuation-drift risk requiring fiscal-year urgency.
- **Don't compete with BroadStreet/Hilb/King Risk** — they buy generalist P&C, not specialty. Their presence is neutral for G&B, not threatening.
- **NY acquisition target filter:** default-exclude NY-domiciled agencies. Acquire Midwest, open NY office post-close.
- **Feed into DealsX sheet for Sam:** Hub is the "do not compete" signal, the broader specialty list remains intact.
